goal: Read leads from Google Sheets, filter to high-qualified leads (stage=4), send a summary HTML table email to the user, and send per-salesperson emails with their assigned qualified leads.

config.description: Google Sheets spreadsheet ID containing leads data
Name of the sheet tab containing leads
Stage value that qualifies a lead as high-qualified
Email address to send the summary to
Subject line for the leads summary email
config.default: Your tasks today - Please handle the following leads - High-qualified leads summary

config.description: Comma-separated list of column names to include in the HTML table
config.default: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person

invariants: Only leads with Stage equal to the configured high_qualified_stage value are included
Header row is the first row; column matching is case-insensitive and whitespace-trimmed
If zero qualified leads exist, a 'no leads found' email is sent instead of an empty table
Each salesperson receives only their own assigned leads
required_outcomes: User receives an email with an HTML table of all high-qualified leads (or a no-leads-found message)
Each salesperson receives an email with an HTML table of their assigned high-qualified leads

steps.summary: Read all rows from the Leads tab in the Google Sheets spreadsheet

Convert raw 2D array into array of objects using the first row as header names, normalizing header names (trim whitespace, case-insensitive matching) to canonical column names: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person

transform.description: First row is headers. Map each subsequent row to an object with keys: date, lead_name, company, email, phone, stage, notes, sales_person. Normalize header matching case-insensitively. Stage values should be parsed as numbers.

steps.summary: Filter leads to only those where stage equals the configured high-qualified stage value (4)

transform.description: Keep only leads where stage equals the high-qualified threshold

steps.summary: Count the number of qualified leads to determine if any exist
Branch based on whether any qualified leads were found

then.summary: Generate an HTML email body with a table of all qualified leads for the user

generate.instruction: Create a professional HTML email body containing a summary table of all qualified leads. The table must have columns: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person. Use proper HTML table markup with <table>, <thead>, <tbody>, styled with borders, header row background color (#4472C4 with white text), cell padding (8px), and alternating row colors for readability. Include a brief greeting at the top like 'Here are your high-qualified leads for today:' and a closing line. The table should list every lead from the input data.
generate.reason: Free-form HTML email body composition with styled table layout requires semantic content generation — no template structure available in transform primitives

outputs.name: html_body
outputs.description: Complete HTML email body with styled leads table

then.summary: Send the full qualified leads summary email to the user
Group qualified leads by the sales_person field to create per-salesperson groups

transform.description: Group leads by the sales_person field. Each group contains the salesperson identifier as key and an array of their assigned leads as items.

key.description: The sales_person value (name or email) that identifies this group

items.description: Array of leads assigned to this salesperson

then.summary: For each salesperson group, generate an HTML table of their leads and send them an email

do.summary: Generate an HTML email body with a table of this salesperson's assigned qualified leads

generate.instruction: Create a professional HTML email body for a salesperson containing a table of their assigned high-qualified leads. The input contains a 'key' (salesperson name/email) and 'items' (array of leads). The table must have columns: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes. Use proper HTML table markup with <table>, <thead>, <tbody>, styled with borders, header row background color (#4472C4 with white text), cell padding (8px), and alternating row colors. Include a greeting addressing the salesperson by name (from the 'key' field) like 'Hi [Name], here are your high-qualified leads for today:' and a closing line encouraging follow-up.
generate.reason: Free-form HTML email body composition with personalized greeting and styled table layout requires semantic content generation

outputs.name: html_body
outputs.description: Complete HTML email body with styled leads table for this salesperson

outputs.name: recipient_identifier
outputs.description: The salesperson identifier (name or email) extracted from the group key

do.summary: Send the per-salesperson qualified leads email

else.summary: Send an email to the user informing them that no high-qualified leads were found

body.value:
Hi,

No high-qualified leads (Stage = 4) were found in the Leads sheet today.

No action is required at this time.

Best regards,
Your Leads Agent

unresolved_questions: The 'Sales Person' column value is used as the email recipient for per-salesperson emails. Please confirm whether this column contains email addresses (e.g., sara@company.com). If it contains names instead, provide a mapping of salesperson names to their email addresses so the workflow can resolve recipients correctly.
assumptions: The Sales Person column contains email addresses that can be used directly as email recipients
Stage values in the sheet are numeric or string representations of numbers (e.g., '4' or 4)
The sheet has exactly 8 columns matching the specified headers: Date, Lead Name, Company, Email, Phone, Stage, Notes, Sales Person
The filter comparison handles both numeric 4 and string '4' for the stage field
